fact: December 13 sits exactly 8 days before the winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere, making it one of the darkest days of the year!
In Alaska, the sun rises after 10 AM and sets before 4 PM on this date, giving kids less than 6 hours of daylight to play outside.
Reindeer in the Arctic are now living in almost complete darkness, relying on their super-sensitive noses to find food buried under snow.
December 13 falls during Sagittarius season in the zodiac calendar.
Pine trees are busy making antifreeze in their needles right now to survive freezing temperatures that could kill other plants.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, December 13 brings blazing summer heat with temperatures that can melt chocolate bars in minutes.
Owls are having their easiest hunting season right now because the long nights give them up to 16 hours of darkness to swoop down on unsuspecting mice.
December 13 is exactly the 347th day of the year, leaving only 18 days until the calendar flips to a brand new year.
Polar bears are at their fluffiest right now, with their winter coats so thick that they can sleep comfortably on ice in minus 40 degree weather.
The Geminids meteor shower typically peaks around December 13, shooting up to 120 colorful space rocks per hour across the night sky.
Deciduous trees have been completely leafless for weeks now, but their roots are still growing slowly underground even in the cold soil.
